content.text: The system shall allow users to navigate to Efficiency Analytics → Environment Efficiency tab.
navigate to the Environment Safety tab under Safety Analytics
I can analyze warehouse safety events
the user is on the application
the user navigates to Safety Analytics → Environment Safety
the system shall load the Environment Safety tab.
The system shall display the page title “Efficiency Analytics”.
I know which module I am in
the Safety Analytics page is displayed
The system shall highlight the “Environment Efficiency” tab as active when selected.
the selected tab to be highlighted
the user selects the Environment Safety tab
the system shall highlight the “Environment Safety” tab.
The system shall load and display a 3D warehouse layout view upon selecting the Environment Productivity tab.
the Environment Safety tab is selected
the system shall display a 3D warehouse layout.
The system shall display all safety events from the last 24 hours by default when the 3D warehouse layout is loaded.
the system shall display safety events from the last 24 hours.
The system shall display an interactive 3D representation of the warehouse layout.
the system shall render an interactive 3D warehouse layout.
The system shall display warehouse elements such as zones, racks, pathways, and operational areas.
the system shall display zones, racks, pathways, and operational areas.
The system shall visually highlight zones using color-coded overlays (e.g., red, green, blue, purple). (on hold)
the system shall apply color-coded overlays.
The system shall update the 3D view dynamically based on applied filters.
the system shall update the 3D view dynamically.
The system shall support zoom in, zoom out, and pan interactions within the 3D view.
the system shall support zoom in, zoom out, and pan.
The system shall display a vertical filter toolbar on the left side.
the Environment Safety screen is displayed
the system shall display a vertical filter toolbar on the left.
The system shall display a “Select Date” input control on the Environment Efficiency screen.
I can filter data by date
the Environment Efficiency screen is displayed
the system shall display a “Select Date” input control.
The system shall open a calendar date picker panel when the user clicks on the “Select Date” control.
the Select Date control is visible
the system shall open a calendar date picker panel.
The system shall display the Current month and year by default in the calendar panel.
the system shall display current month and year by default.
The system shall allow the user to navigate between months using previous and next arrow controls.
the user clicks previous or next arrows
the system shall navigate between months.
The system shall display all days of the selected month in a grid format (Sunday to Saturday).
the system shall display days in a Sunday–Saturday grid.
The system shall highlight the current date by default.
the system shall highlight the current date.
The system shall allow the user to select a specific date by clicking on a date cell.
the user clicks on a date cell
the system shall select that date.
The system shall visually highlight the selected date in the calendar.
the system shall highlight the selected date.
The system shall update the selected date value in the “Select Date” field after selection.
selected date to appear in input
the system shall update the “Select Date” field.
The system shall automatically refresh Environment Efficiency data based on the selected date.
the system shall refresh Environment Efficiency data.
The system shall allow the user to close the calendar panel by selecting a date or clicking outside the panel.
the user selects a date or clicks outside
the system shall close the calendar panel.
The system shall display an MHE Filter panel when the user clicks on the MHE filter icon.
I can filter warehouse data by equipment
the Environment Safety screen is displayed
the user clicks on the MHE filter icon
the system shall display the MHE filter panel.
The system shall display a list of available MHE assets within the filter panel.
the MHE filter panel is open
the system shall display a list of available MHE assets.
Each MHE item shall include a toggle control (on/off switch) for selection.
I can enable or disable it
the system shall display a toggle control for each MHE.
The system shall allow users to enable or disable individual MHEs using the toggle control.
the system shall enable or disable that MHE.
The system shall display the MHE name and type (e.g., Electric Forklift, Stacker, Reach Truck) for each item.
the system shall display MHE name and type.
The system shall provide a master toggle to enable or disable all MHEs at once.
the MHE filter panel is open
the user uses the master toggle
the system shall enable or disable all MHEs.
The system shall support scroll functionality within the MHE filter panel when the list exceeds the visible area.
the MHE list exceeds visible area
the system shall support vertical scrolling.

30.title: Update 3D View Based on MHE Filter

content.text: The system shall dynamically update the 3D warehouse view based on selected MHEs.
the system shall update the 3D warehouse view.
The system shall visually indicate active (enabled) and inactive (disabled) MHEs.
the system shall indicate active and inactive states.
The system shall allow the filter panel to be collapsed or closed when not in use.
I can focus on the view
the user closes or collapses it
the system shall hide the panel.
The system shall display an Operator filter panel when the user clicks on the Operator filter icon.
I can filter data by operators
the Environment Safety screen is displayed
the user clicks the Operator filter icon
the system shall display the Operator filter panel.
The system shall display a list of operators including Operator Name and unique identifier (e.g., ID/code).
the system shall display operator name and ID.
The system shall provide a toggle switch for each operator to enable or disable selection.
the system shall display toggle switches.
The system shall provide a master toggle to select or deselect all operators at once.
the system shall select or deselect all operators.
The system shall allow multiple operator selection simultaneously.
the system shall allow multiple selection.
The system shall display all operators as enabled (selected) by default.
all operators shall be enabled by default.

39.title: Update View Based on Operator Filter

content.text: The system shall dynamically filter and update the 3D warehouse visualization based on selected operators.
the system shall update the 3D visualization.
The system shall hide operator-related movements/events in the 3D view when an operator is deselected.
the system shall hide that operator’s data.
The system shall support vertical scrolling within the operator list when the number of operators exceeds the visible area.
the system shall support vertical scrolling.
The system shall allow the user to reopen and modify operator selections at any time.
The system shall ensure real-time or near real-time update of the visualization upon selection/deselection.
the system shall update visualization in real-time or near real-time.
The system shall display a filter panel titled “Zones.”
the Environment Efficiency screen is displayed
the user views the filter section
the system shall display a panel titled “Zones”.
The system shall display a master toggle switch in the Zones panel header to enable or disable visibility of all zones.
I can enable or disable all zones
the system shall display a master toggle switch.

46.title: Enable All Zones via Master Toggle

content.text: When the Zones master toggle is enabled, the system shall display all available zones on the canvas.
I can view all zones on the canvas
the system shall display all zones on the canvas.

47.title: Disable All Zones via Master Toggle

content.text: When the Zones master toggle is disabled, the system shall hide all zone layers from the canvas visualization.
the system shall hide all zones from the canvas.
The system shall display a list of individual zones within the Zones panel (e.g., W1, MHE Path 1, Office, Aisle A, Aisle B, Aisle C, Dock).
the system shall display a list of zones.
The system shall display a color indicator next to each zone representing the zone type or category.
the system shall display a colour indicator for each zone.
The system shall display a toggle switch next to each zone name to control the visibility of that specific zone.
the system shall display a toggle switch for each zone.
When the user enables the toggle for a zone, the system shall display that zone on the canvas visualization.
I can view it on the canvas
the system shall display that zone on the canvas.
When the user disables the toggle for a zone, the system shall hide that zone from the canvas visualization.
I can hide it from the canvas
the system shall hide that zone from the canvas.
The system shall display the zone type or classification below the zone name (e.g., Pedestrian circulation, MHE circulation, Aisle).
the system shall display zone type below the name.
The system shall support scrolling within the Zones panel when the number of zones exceeds the visible panel height.
the zone list exceeds visible area
the system shall support vertical scrolling.
The system shall dynamically update the canvas visualization when zone visibility settings are changed.
the system shall update the canvas visualization dynamically.
The system shall display a filter panel titled “Trip Types.”
see the Trip Types filter panel
I can filter trips by type
the Environment Efficiency screen is displayed
the user views the filter section
the system shall display a panel titled “Trip Types”.
The system shall display trip type options within the panel, including Loaded, Empty, and Partially Loaded.
the Trip Types panel is displayed
the system shall display Loaded, Empty, and Partially Loaded options.

58.title: View Colour Indicators for Trip Types

content.text: The system shall display a color indicator for each trip type representing the event classification.
colour indicators for each trip type
the system shall display a colour indicator for each trip type.

59.title: View Toggle Controls for Trip Types

content.text: The system shall display a toggle control next to each trip type option.
toggle controls for each trip type
I can enable or disable them
the system shall display toggle controls next to each option.
When the user enables a trip type toggle, the system shall display trips of that type within the visualization.
a trip type toggle is available
the system shall display trips of that type in the visualization.
When the user disables a trip type toggle, the system shall hide trips of that type from the visualization.
a trip type toggle is enabled
the system shall hide trips of that type from the visualization.

62.title: Dynamic Update of Trip Type Filters

content.text: The system shall dynamically refresh the visualization layer when trip type filters are changed.
the system shall refresh the visualization layer dynamically.
The system shall display a filter panel titled “Cycle Time.”
the system shall display “Cycle Time” panel.
The system shall display cycle time ranges as selectable filter options.
the system shall display cycle time ranges.
The system shall display the following cycle time ranges: 0–5 min, 6–10 min, 11–15 min, 16–20 min, 21–25 min, and 25+ min.
the system shall display ranges (0–5, 6–10, 11–15, etc.).
The system shall display each cycle time range as a selectable filter button within the panel.
the system shall display selectable buttons.
The system shall allow the user to select only one cycle time range at a time.
the system shall allow only one selection.
The system shall visually highlight selected cycle time ranges.
the system shall highlight selected button.

69.title: Filter Data Based on Cycle Time

content.text: The system shall filter pallet movement or cycle time data on the visualization canvas based on the selected cycle time ranges.
The system shall dynamically update the visualization when cycle time filters are applied or removed.
the system shall update visualization dynamically.
The system shall deselect the selected cycle time range when the user clicks the “Clear” button within the Cycle Time filter panel.
the system shall deselect the range.
The system shall provide a show Heatmap option.
the Environment Safety screen is displayed
the system shall provide a heatmap option.
The system shall provide a Heatmap icon/button to enable or disable heatmap visualization on the 3D warehouse layout.
the user clicks the heatmap icon
the system shall enable or disable heatmap.
The system shall display a heatmap overlay on the warehouse layout when the heatmap is enabled.
the system shall show heatmap overlay.
The system shall represent event density/intensity using colour gradients (e.g., low → light colour, high → dark/red colour).
the system shall use colour gradients (low to high intensity).
The system shall calculate heatmap intensity based on the frequency of events occurring in specific locations.
the system shall calculate intensity based on event frequency of events occurring in specific locations.
The system shall dynamically update the heatmap based on selected filters (Date Range, Operator, MHE, Events).
filters (Date, Operator, MHE, Events) are applied
the system shall update the heatmap.

78.title: Display Heatmap for Selected Time Range

content.text: The system shall display heatmap data corresponding only to the selected time range (default: last 24 hours).
the system shall show data for that time range.
The system shall allow the user to toggle off the heatmap icon, reverting to the normal 3D view.
the system shall revert to standard 3D view.
The system shall update the heatmap in real-time or near real-time when filter selections are changed.
the system shall update heatmap in real-time.
The system shall ensure smooth rendering and performance optimization for large datasets.
I can use the system efficiently
the system shall ensure smooth performance.
The system shall display a bottom toolbar within the 3D warehouse view containing interactive control icons.
the system shall display a bottom toolbar with control icons.
The system shall ensure the toolbar is fixed at the bottom centre of the screen and remains visible during navigation.
the toolbar shall remain fixed at the bottom center.
The system shall visually indicate the active/selected state of controls (e.g., highlighted icon).
the system shall highlight the active icon.
The system shall execute all toolbar actions in real-time or near real-time without requiring page reload.
the system shall execute it in real-time.
The system shall provide a 2D View toggle button to switch from 3D view to a top-down orthographic view.
the system shall switch to 2D view.
The system shall display a tooltip (e.g., “2D View”) when the user hovers over the control icon.
the user hovers over the 2D icon
the system shall display tooltip “2D View”.
The system shall provide a 3D View toggle button to switch back from 2D view to 3D perspective view.
the system shall switch to 3D view.
The system shall display a First-Person View (FPV) icon in the bottom-left toolbar of the Rack Health Analytics screen.
the system shall display FPV icon.
When the user clicks the First-Person View icon, the system shall switch the workspace camera to first-person navigation mode.
the system shall switch to first-person mode.
Upon entering First-Person View, the system shall display an on-screen instruction tooltip indicating how to enable mouse look.
the system shall display instruction tooltip.
The system shall require the user to click within the viewport to enable mouse look for camera rotation.
the system shall enable mouse look.
While in First-Person View, the system shall allow keyboard-based movement controls using W, A, S, and D keys.
the user presses W, A, S, D keys
the system shall move the camera accordingly.
The system shall allow the user to sprint while navigating in First-Person View using the Shift key.
the system shall enable sprint movement.

95.title: View FPV (First Person View) Control Guide

content.text: The system shall display an on-screen control guide indicating movement keys, sprint action, and mouse release instructions.
the system shall display an on-screen control guide (movement keys, sprint, mouse instructions).

96.title: Release Mouse Control in FPV (First Person View)

content.text: The system shall allow the user to release mouse control using the Esc key without exiting First-Person View.
the system shall release mouse control without exiting FPV.
The system shall display a clearly visible “Exit First-Person View” action button while FPV mode is active.
the system shall display “Exit First-Person View” button.
When the user clicks Exit First-Person View, the system shall return the camera to the previous layout or default view mode.
the system shall return to previous/default view.
The system shall ensure First-Person View navigation respects physical layout boundaries (e.g., floors, walls, rack geometry).
I don’t move outside valid areas
the system shall restrict movement within floors, walls, and rack geometry.
The system shall maintain real-time rendering performance during First-Person navigation to support smooth movement.
the system shall maintain real-time rendering performance.
The First-Person View mode shall be read-only, preventing layout edits or analytics modifications while active.
the user attempts any edit action
the system shall prevent layout or analytics modifications.
The system shall provide an Auto Fit control to automatically adjust zoom and position to fit the entire warehouse layout within the viewport.
I can see the entire layout
the system shall adjust zoom and position to fit the layout.

103.title: Trigger Auto Fit via Keyboard Shortcut

content.text: The system shall support a keyboard shortcut (e.g., “F”) to trigger Auto Fit functionality.
the system shall trigger Auto Fit.
The system shall provide a Full screen toggle control to expand the 3D viewer to full screen.
the system shall expand the viewer to full screen.
The system shall allow exiting full screen via the same control or keyboard shortcut (e.g., F11).
the user clicks control or presses F11
the system shall exit full screen.
Upon clicking the Fullscreen control, the system shall expand the 3D viewer to occupy the entire screen, hiding browser and application UI elements where applicable.
I can focus on the layout
the system shall hide browser/app UI and occupy entire screen.
The system shall provide a control to Gray out rack structures to reduce visual clutter.
the system shall gray out rack structures.
The system shall allow toggling back to original rack colours.
the system shall restore original colours.
The system shall provide a Geometry Filters panel when the filter icon is clicked.
the system shall display Geometry Filters panel.
the system shall display Site, Building, Structure, Zones, and Layouts.
The system shall display expand/collapse arrow controls next to geometry elements that contain child elements within the Geometry Filters hierarchy.
the system shall display expand/collapse arrows.
The system shall expand the selected geometry node when the user clicks the expand arrow icon.
the system shall expand and display child elements.
The system shall collapse the selected geometry node when the user clicks the collapse arrow icon.
the user clicks the collapse arrow
the system shall collapse the node.

114.title: Expand Site Node to View Buildings

content.text: When the user expands the site node (e.g., “pune-ind”), the system shall display the list of buildings associated with that site (e.g., Building 1).
the user clicks the expand arrow
the system shall display the list of buildings under the site.

115.title: Expand Building Node to View Categories

content.text: When the user expands the building node (e.g., “Building 1”), the system shall display the child geometry categories under that building such as Structure and Zones.
I can view structure and zones
the user clicks the expand arrow
the system shall display Structure and Zones categories.
When the user expands the “Structure” node, the system shall display the list of structural elements associated with that building, such as Walls and Floors.
the user clicks the expand arrow
the system shall display Walls and Floors.
When the user expands the “Zones” node, the system shall display the list of zones defined in the facility layout (e.g., MHE Path 1, Aisle A, Aisle B, Aisle C, Dock).
the user clicks the expand arrow
the system shall display zone list (e.g., aisles, paths, dock).
When the user collapses a parent node, the system shall hide all child elements under that node from the filter panel.
the system shall hide all child elements.
The system shall maintain the selection state of child elements even when the parent node is collapsed.
the system shall retain selection state.
The system shall support multi-level expansion within the hierarchy to allow users to navigate through nested geometry elements.
the system shall support multi-level expansion.
The system shall allow users to select/deselect elements using checkboxes.
the system shall allow selection and deselection.

122.title: Update 3D View Based on Geometry Filters

content.text: The system shall update the 3D view dynamically based on selected geometry filters.
the system shall update the 3D warehouse view dynamically.
The system shall allow the user to close the geometry filters panel using the x icon in the header of the panel or by clicking anywhere outside the panel.
I can focus on the layout
the Geometry Filters panel is open
the user clicks the “X” icon or outside the panel
the system shall close the panel.
